system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"availability.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful health checks returning JSON {\"status\":\"ok\"}.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.health_200_json_status_ok"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Direct signal of basic service availability and correct health endpoint behavior; useful for alerting and release validation."},{"key":"reliability.global_500_error_rate","description":"Rate of unhandled internal server errors returned as 500 with JSON error.","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.global_error_500_internal_server_error"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Primary indicator of unexpected failures impacting users; should be monitored across deploys and traffic changes."},{"key":"api.todos_list_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful list-todos responses returning a JSON array (200).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos_list_200_json_array"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Measures reliability of a core read path; regressions here often indicate DB/connectivity/serialization issues."},{"key":"api.todos_create_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful todo creation responses (201 with created todo JSON).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos_create_201_created_todo"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Measures reliability of a core write path; sensitive to validation, DB writes, and schema changes."},{"key":"api.todos_update_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful todo update responses (200 with updated todo JSON).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos_update_200_updated_todo"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Tracks correctness and stability of update behavior; catches regressions in patch/merge logic and persistence."},{"key":"api.todos_delete_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful todo deletion responses (204 No Content).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos_delete_204_no_content"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Ensures delete path remains functional; useful for detecting authorization/routing/DB issues."},{"key":"api.todos_create_validation_error_ratio","description":"Ratio of create requests rejected due to missing/empty title (400) relative to successful creates (201) plus this validation error.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos_create_400_title_required","http.todos_create_201_created_todo"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Monitors client/input quality and potential UI/API contract regressions; spikes can indicate breaking changes or frontend bugs."},{"key":"api.todos_update_empty_body_error_ratio","description":"Ratio of update requests rejected due to no fields to update (400) relative to successful updates (200) plus this validation error.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos_update_400_no_fields_to_update","http.todos_update_200_updated_todo"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Detects malformed client behavior or API contract drift; helps distinguish user error from server reliability issues."},{"key":"api.todos_get_not_found_ratio","description":"Ratio of get-by-id requests resulting in 404 not found relative to successful list responses plus get 404s (proxy for lookup miss rate).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos_get_404_todo_not_found","http.todos_list_200_json_array"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Tracks how often clients request missing resources; sudden changes can indicate ID generation issues, caching bugs, or data loss."},{"key":"api.todos_update_not_found_ratio","description":"Ratio of update attempts on non-existent todos (404) relative to successful updates (200) plus update 404s.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos_update_404_todo_not_found","http.todos_update_200_updated_todo"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Highlights stale client state or concurrency issues; increases can indicate data consistency problems or incorrect routing/ID usage."},{"key":"api.todos_delete_not_found_ratio","description":"Ratio of delete attempts on non-existent todos (404) relative to successful deletes (204) plus delete 404s.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos_delete_404_todo_not_found","http.todos_delete_204_no_content"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Monitors client correctness and data lifecycle issues; spikes can indicate double-deletes, race conditions, or UI state bugs."},{"key":"startup.backend_running_log_count","description":"Count of startup log events indicating the backend is running on a port.","direction":"min","compute":"count","inputs":["log.backend_running_on_port"],"segment_dimension":"deployment_id","rationale":"Validates that instances are starting and reaching the point of accepting traffic; useful for rollout/boot diagnostics."},{"key":"startup.port_file_write_count","description":"Count of successful writes of the chosen backend port to /tmp/todo-backend-port.","direction":"min","compute":"count","inputs":["file.write_tmp_todo_backend_port"],"segment_dimension":"deployment_id","rationale":"Ensures frontend discovery mechanism is functioning; missing events can cause partial outages even if the backend is healthy."},{"key":"startup.discovery_write_to_log_ratio","description":"Ratio of port file writes to backend startup log events (port announced).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["file.write_tmp_todo_backend_port","log.backend_running_on_port"],"segment_dimension":"deployment_id","rationale":"Detects mismatches where the service starts but fails to publish discovery info; helps isolate integration/startup regressions."}]}

gates.description: Rate of successful health checks returning JSON {"status":"ok"}.
gates.rationale: Direct signal of basic service availability and correct health endpoint behavior; useful for alerting and release validation.

gates.description: Rate of unhandled internal server errors returned as 500 with JSON error.
gates.rationale: Primary indicator of unexpected failures impacting users; should be monitored across deploys and traffic changes.

gates.description: Rate of successful list-todos responses returning a JSON array (200).
gates.rationale: Measures reliability of a core read path; regressions here often indicate DB/connectivity/serialization issues.

gates.description: Rate of successful todo creation responses (201 with created todo JSON).
gates.rationale: Measures reliability of a core write path; sensitive to validation, DB writes, and schema changes.

gates.description: Rate of successful todo update responses (200 with updated todo JSON).
gates.rationale: Tracks correctness and stability of update behavior; catches regressions in patch/merge logic and persistence.

gates.description: Rate of successful todo deletion responses (204 No Content).
gates.rationale: Ensures delete path remains functional; useful for detecting authorization/routing/DB issues.

gates.description: Ratio of create requests rejected due to missing/empty title (400) relative to successful creates (201) plus this validation error.
gates.rationale: Monitors client/input quality and potential UI/API contract regressions; spikes can indicate breaking changes or frontend bugs.

gates.description: Ratio of update requests rejected due to no fields to update (400) relative to successful updates (200) plus this validation error.
gates.rationale: Detects malformed client behavior or API contract drift; helps distinguish user error from server reliability issues.

gates.description: Ratio of get-by-id requests resulting in 404 not found relative to successful list responses plus get 404s (proxy for lookup miss rate).
gates.rationale: Tracks how often clients request missing resources; sudden changes can indicate ID generation issues, caching bugs, or data loss.

gates.description: Ratio of update attempts on non-existent todos (404) relative to successful updates (200) plus update 404s.
gates.rationale: Highlights stale client state or concurrency issues; increases can indicate data consistency problems or incorrect routing/ID usage.

gates.description: Ratio of delete attempts on non-existent todos (404) relative to successful deletes (204) plus delete 404s.
gates.rationale: Monitors client correctness and data lifecycle issues; spikes can indicate double-deletes, race conditions, or UI state bugs.

gates.description: Count of startup log events indicating the backend is running on a port.
gates.rationale: Validates that instances are starting and reaching the point of accepting traffic; useful for rollout/boot diagnostics.

gates.description: Count of successful writes of the chosen backend port to /tmp/todo-backend-port.
gates.rationale: Ensures frontend discovery mechanism is functioning; missing events can cause partial outages even if the backend is healthy.

gates.description: Ratio of port file writes to backend startup log events (port announced).
gates.rationale: Detects mismatches where the service starts but fails to publish discovery info; helps isolate integration/startup regressions.
